title: Increase Transolver block mlp_ratio 2->4

test_geom_camber_cruise.note: NaN — bad ground truth y[p] on test_geom_camber_cruise idx 20 + scoring.py NaN-mask bug

decision_reason: test_avg/mae_surf_p NaN; later corrected diagnosis: scoring.py mask is defeated by NaN*0 propagation, fix is in train.py:evaluate_split (now in baseline post #356). Nezuko instructed to rebase + retain mlp_ratio=4.

title: EMA(0.999) shadow weights for val + checkpoint selection
decision_reason: First clean baseline on this branch. EMA gave -3.1% on val_avg/mae_surf_p (predicted -2% to -7%). NaN-safe pre-pass in evaluate_split is a real bug-find against scoring.py and is now in baseline. Merged 23:42 UTC, commit 208f1cc.

title: Raise surface loss weight 10->50 (rerun on post-#356 EMA baseline)
decision_reason: Wash (-0.95% val, -0.12% test) on the post-#356 baseline they rebased onto; clear regression vs current post-#374 baseline because grad-clip merged after rebase started. EMA already absorbs most of the surface-signal gain - marginal value of surf_weight=50 on top is within noise. Volume MAE didn't blow up (model isn't catastrophically forgetting volume). Askeladd's own recommendation: don't merge. Reassigned to PR #417 (ema-decay-0p99) to address nezuko's 'EMA decay too slow at 13-ep budget' diagnostic.

title: Raise surface loss weight 10->50

test_geom_camber_cruise.note: NaN due to scoring.py + bad y[p] sample idx 20

decision_reason: val 135.19 +2.2% vs new baseline 132.28 (not >5% regression so not closing). Run was raw-only and pre-baseline-NaN-fix. Sent back to rebase onto post-#356 baseline (EMA + nan-safe), keep surf_weight=50, test compounding.

title: Double slice tokens (64->128) and heads (4->8)
test_avg_note: computed via student's NaN-safe rerun; original train.py test eval reported NaN due to scoring.py inf*0=NaN bug
decision_reason: Clear >5% regression on both val (+18.3%) and test (+22.1%) vs new baseline 132.276/118.041. Cause is throughput (250s/ep => only 8/50 epochs in 30-min budget) not the lever itself. Frieren reassigned to PR #373 (mixed-slice-last-layer).

title: SmoothL1 (Huber, beta=1) on surface loss; keep MSE on volume
decision_reason: Decisive winner (-20.2% on val_avg/mae_surf_p vs EMA baseline; raw-vs-raw -22.7%). Sent back due to evaluate_split conflict with merged #356, not lever quality. Rebase resolution: take baseline's evaluate_split, keep edward's SmoothL1 in the loss block. Will merge as new baseline once clean post-rebase numbers land. Predicted SmoothL1+EMA: val ~100-102, test ~92-95.

title: 5-epoch linear warmup + cosine to 1e-5 with peak LR=1e-3

test_geom_camber_cruise.note: NaN due to scoring.py + bad y[p] sample idx 20

decision_reason: Pre-EMA, pre-grad-clip base. Raw 139.91 vs #356 raw best 136.53 (+2.5%) and vs #374 raw best 121.99 (+14.7%). Schedule was degenerate at the actual budget (cosine T_max=50, only 7 cosine epochs ran, LR barely decayed from 1e-3). Peak lr=1e-3 trained stably (no NaN, smooth train loss) but val oscillated. Reassigned fern to PR #408 (higher-lr-1e3) as single-knob test on the merged grad-clip baseline.

title: Larger batch (4->8) + sqrt(2) LR scaling (5e-4->7e-4)
decision_reason: Clear >5% regression on both val (+75%) and test (+74%) vs current baseline. Mechanism: step-count starvation dominates -- batch=8 halves optimizer steps per epoch (188 vs 375), sqrt(2) LR scaling under-compensates (integrated LR ~0.77x baseline). Variance reduction confirmed (grad_norm -16%) but eaten by missing late-training updates. single_in_dist canary regressed +99-102% as the hardest, most-steps-hungry split. Frieren reassigned to PR #458 (weight-decay-5e-4).

title: Lion lr 1.7e-4 -> 2.5e-4 (bracket-narrowing the basin upper edge)
decision_reason: Strict merge gate satisfied on val (-1.67% vs current). Test +0.65% within run-to-run noise. Squash-merge composes cleanly (lr in Config + β in loss block — different code regions; git auto-merge applies both). 11th merge; 4th Lion-axis lever. Lion's basin upper edge confirmed in [2.5e-4, 3.3e-4]. Per-split uniform improvement; mean grad-norm -30% (Lion finds flatter regions faster at higher lr). Tanjiro reassigned to PR #592 (lr=2.85e-4) for bracket-narrowing midpoint.

title: GeGLU at matched params (silu -> gelu in gated MLP)
decision_reason: Clean win on run-base #352 (-2.62%/-3.27%). But #535 (β=0.5) merged before results posted; vs current +1.57%/+3.37% (within 5% close threshold but past merge gate). Branch has β=1.0; squash would revert merged β=0.5. PER-SPLIT signature (single -7.91%, re_rand -2.19%, cruise +1.42%) FALSIFIES 'any gating works' hypothesis -- activation shape is load-bearing. Sent back for rebase + re-run on post-#535. Both GeGLU and β=0.5 target high-Re tail via different mechanisms; post-rebase will test compound/subsume/interfere.

title: Lion + batch=8 (fell back to b=6 after OOM)
diagnostic_lion_vs_adamw_interaction: Under AdamW (#403) b=8 + sqrt(2)LR was catastrophic (+169% val); under Lion b=6 we land within statistical noise of baseline. Lion's bounded sign-update decouples per-step magnitude from batch size.
decision_reason: +4.11% val / +5.98% test vs current #535 (just past close threshold on test). Branch has SmoothL1 beta=1.0; squash-merge would revert merged beta=0.5 and undo #535's win -- structurally incompatible. Predicted 'smoother momentum buffer at higher batch' didn't materialize (spread unchanged). Per-split redistribution rather than uniform improvement. b=8 OOM means original hypothesis untestable without bf16. Reassigned to PR #580 (lion-lr-1p2e-4) -- lower-edge probe complementing tanjiro's #536 upper-edge.

title: Lion betas = (0.9, 0.99) -> (0.95, 0.99) (slower direction signal)
decision_reason: Clear regression. Frieren's 8th excellent write-up: WIN MECHANISM (smoother direction -> smoother iterate) confirmed (raw eps 9-12 monotone vs #352's bouncy, mean grad-norm -25%, EMA-raw spread shrunk). LOSE MECHANISM (slower convergence under fixed budget) dominated -- EMA gap from #352 widens monotonically through training. PER-SPLIT: single_in_dist (stationary-gradient regime) WON -1.97% val; tandem splits (non-stationary) LOST 8-16%. 'Stationary regimes prefer inertia, non-stationary regimes prefer responsiveness.' Reassigned to PR #571 (lion-beta2-0p999) -- buffer-side test.

title: SmoothL1 beta=1.0 -> 0.5 (narrow L1-regime threshold)
decision_reason: Past merge gate vs current baseline. PER-SPLIT SIGNATURE INVERSION: at beta=1.0 cruise was dominant winner (#352); at beta=0.5 single_in_dist becomes dominant winner (-8.3%/-13.3%) with re_rand close behind. The beta knob redirects SmoothL1's per-split benefit between cruise (beta=1.0) and single (beta=0.5). 'L1-tail captures high-magnitude residuals' mechanism re-asserts. 9th merge; second loss-form refinement. Merged 05:27 commit 1223c15.

title: TF32 matmul precision (rebased onto post-#352 Lion+SmoothL1 baseline)
decision_reason: Strict merge rule satisfied. Throughput multiplier is the durable win: -13% per-epoch (14 epochs in budget vs 12), optimizer-agnostic. Per-split: 3/4 val improve, 2/4 test improve; single_in_dist big winner (-6.8% val) from 2 extra cosine epochs benefiting in-distribution learning; rc regresses (+3-5%, within run-to-run variance). Permanent throughput floor for all round-2 PRs. Merged 05:17 commit f8d8ffb. Earlier prior-rebase (post-#398 base) closed (sent back) without metrics update; this PR is the rebased-onto-#352 entry.

title: TF32 matmul precision -- single-line throughput
decision_reason: Beats #398 base (-3.20%/-3.02%) but Lion #430 merged before results posted moving baseline to 67.737. Throughput delivery durable (-13% per-epoch), optimizer-agnostic. Predicted post-#430 rebase: val ~63-66, test ~55-58.

title: Dropout p=0.1 inside SwiGLUMLP.forward
diagnostic_ep9_crossover: Dropout strictly better through ep8, strictly worse from ep10. geom_camber_rc improved -0.5%; cruise (closest-to-noise-floor) regressed +9%.
decision_reason: Mild regression on run base (+3.97% val) and clear regression vs current Lion baseline (+37.18%). Frieren's diagnostic: dropout helps early-epoch under-fit but late-epoch noise penalty exceeds gain at p=0.1. Reassigned to PR #513 (dropout p=0.05) -- narrow the bracket per frieren's own follow-up #1.

title: SwiGLU swiglu_inner 168 -> 256 (+50% MLP, +25% total)
decision_reason: Clear regression vs both #398 base (+5.08%) and current Lion #430 (+38.61%). Nezuko's diagnostic: training-budget starvation not OOD-overfit. Even single_in_dist regressed (+8.46%), killing closed-#355 in-dist-memorization hypothesis. Per-epoch curve still descending hard at ep12. SwiGLU's gating-fixes-OOD property is at matched-param count, not capacity-on-top. Reassigned to PR #514 (swiglu_inner=192) -- narrower probe.

title: Lion optimizer (rebased onto post-#398 SwiGLU baseline)
decision_reason: Biggest single-PR delta on this branch (-24.19% val, -24.94% test). Beats every val and test split by >=21%. Wall-clock parity (+0.8%). Lion's sign-update + EMA(0.99) compose better than predicted: EMA-Lion interaction at decay 0.99 averages over Lion's substantial epoch-to-epoch raw variance, contributing to the gain. Mechanism well-understood. Merged 03:46 commit 5b2f7b2; new baseline.

title: torch.compile rebased onto post-#398 SwiGLU baseline
decision_reason: Beats #398 base cleanly (-13.51% val, -14.77% test, all splits -12 to -15%). But Lion #430 merged ~5min after results posted, moving baseline to val=67.737. Throughput delivery durable across 3 rebases (-23.1%, -23.0%, -25.7%). Predicted post-#430 rebase: val ~58-63, test ~52-56.

title: SmoothL1 (Huber beta=1) on surface (rebased onto post-#430 Lion)
decision_reason: Beats #430 baseline by -5.28% val / -5.92% test. SmoothL1 lever survives optimizer-family change. PER-SPLIT GAIN REDISTRIBUTION: under AdamW SmoothL1 helped single_in_dist most (high-Re tail); under Lion the gain shifts to cruise/re_rand (Lion's sign-update absorbs the high-Re-tail benefit; SmoothL1 helps via momentum-buffer's sign trajectory). 8th merge on this branch; first compounding loss-form lever. Merged 04:33 commit cb356ed.

title: SwiGLU swiglu_inner 168 -> 192 (+14% MLP, +7% total)
decision_reason: Past close threshold vs current #352. Combined with closed #475: clean two-point SwiGLU capacity curve, 168 (best) < 192 (wash) < 256 (lose). 168 is the local optimum at 30-min/12-epoch budget under Lion. Capacity-on-top doesn't help under Lion at this budget. rc split regressed worst at +14% MLP -- capacity isn't helping under-fit OOD splits. Reassigned nezuko to PR #552 (GeGLU at matched params) -- gating-activation A/B test.

title: Dropout p=0.1 -> 0.05 inside SwiGLUMLP.forward
decision_reason: Mild regression on run base, clear regression vs current. Frieren's 6th write-up: dropout is dead under SwiGLU+Lion at this budget. Bracket: p=0 winning, p=0.05 +1.27%, p=0.1 +3.97% -- monotone regression. Lion's sign-update mutes both win and lose dropout mechanisms toward zero. Per-split asymmetry stable: cruise (closest-to-noise-floor) regressed worst across both p values. Reassigned to PR #545 (lion-beta1-0p95) -- fresh axis after 6 closures.

title: EMA decay 0.99 -> 0.97 on SwiGLU base
decision_reason: Clear regression. EMA-decay axis fully locked at 0.99 across both GELU and SwiGLU bases. Cross-architecture: SwiGLU's smoother gradients reduce value of 'faster shadow tracking'. Cruise sensitivity stable across decays (+16% at 0.95, +16% at 0.97 -- canary for balanced-domain sampler noise floor on smallest-sample-domain). Reassigned to PR #546 (lion-batch-8) -- fresh batch-side probe under Lion.

title: Lion lr 1.7e-4 -> 3.3e-4
decision_reason: Clear regression (+8.45% val, +6.10% test) on the Lion-basin probe. Tanjiro's diagnostic: raw floor rises faster than EMA can smooth at higher Lion lr. Mean pre-clip grad-norm dropped -34% but parameter trajectory lands in worse basin. Lion's basin under our recipe is narrower than AdamW-equivalent heuristic suggests; bracket: 1.7e-4 in basin, 3.3e-4 past it. Reassigned to PR #536 (lion-lr-2p5e-4) -- bracket-narrowing midpoint.

title: SmoothL1 (Huber beta=1) on surface (rebased onto post-#417)
decision_reason: Beats #398 baseline (-7.61%/-7.85%), but Lion #430 merged ~7min before results posted moving baseline to val=67.737. Sent back for rebase + re-run on Lion baseline. Open question: does SmoothL1 still help under Lion's sign-update? Lion's per-step update is invariant to gradient magnitude; honest band -1% to +2%. Even a wash is appendix data.

title: Cosine T_max=13 + eta_min=1e-5 (anneal in budget)
decision_reason: Clear regression vs current baseline. Smoking gun: train loss REVERSED direction at ep13 (schedule un-trained the model). Best raw arrived at ep12 (anneal too aggressive). Effective LR-time-integral 57% of baseline T_max=50 -> 43% less optimization work. Closed-PR insight chain (#353, #438, #465) complete: model needs more high-LR steps, not better anneal. LR/schedule axis fully mapped at this budget. Reassigned fern to PR #491 (tf32-matmul-precision).

title: Weight decay 1e-4 -> 5e-4
decision_reason: Clear regression vs current baseline (+15.59% val, +14.61% test). Predicted lose-case fired: under-converged 13-epoch budget, train losses still falling 15% per epoch at ep13 (no over-fitting to regularize against). geom_camber_rc -- the highest-loss / hardest / most-under-fit split -- regressed worst (+8.14% val), opposite of the predicted OOD-helps pattern. WD-bump direction fully mapped at this budget. Reassigned frieren to PR #483 (swiglu-mlp-dropout-0p1) to test training-time stochasticity as alternative regularizer.

title: EMA decay 0.99 -> 0.95
decision_reason: Clear regression on both val (+9.77%) and test (+10.24%). Mechanism: predicted lose-case fired -- 0.95 half-life ~14 batches insufficient to average out balanced-domain sampler noise (3 domains x ~5 samples). Mean spread fell from 24.5 (#417 at 0.99) to 17.3 (this PR at 0.95). Cross-over with 0.99 at ep5-7. Cruise hardest hit (+16.66% val) -- smallest-sample domain. EMA-decay optimum bracketed [0.97, 0.99]. Reassigned askeladd to PR #474 (ema-decay-0p97).

title: SwiGLU MLP at matched param count (rebased onto post-#417)
decision_reason: First architectural win on this branch after five variance-reduction merges. Beats baseline on ALL val and test splits (per-split max gain -15.6% on single_in_dist). Composes more than additively with the variance-reduction stack (predicted band 94-98, observed 89.35). Surf/vol balance preserved; no in-dist-helps-but-OOD-regresses pathology. Merged 02:48 commit 7fb09c0; new baseline.

title: torch.compile (rebased onto post-#417)
decision_reason: Beats baseline thorfinn rebased onto (#417, val -2.88% / test -4.57%). But PR #398 (SwiGLU) merged ~1 minute before results posted, moving baseline to val=89.35 / test=79.19; this run now sits at +7.15% / +5.90% vs current. Throughput delivery (-23.0% per-epoch, 15 epochs in 30 min) is rock-solid across rebases. Sent back for rebase #2 onto post-#398. Predicted post-rebase: val ~84-88, test ~74-78.

title: Higher LR (1e-3 -> 2e-3) under grad-clip(0.5) envelope
decision_reason: Clear >5% regression on both val and test (apples-to-apples vs #408 +6.75% / +8.33%). Mechanism: AdamW preconditioner only adapts ~24% of doubled LR (vs ~50% from 5e-4->1e-3); raw curve has noise spikes at ep10; EMA(0.999) crossover with raw delayed by 1 epoch and shadow never catches up to baseline trajectory. LR ceiling for max_norm=0.5 envelope now bracketed: 1e-3 wins, 2e-3 loses. Fern reassigned to PR #465 (cosine-tmax-13).

title: Lion optimizer (sign-of-momentum) replacing AdamW
decision_reason: Biggest single-PR signal yet (-19.43% val / -20.26% test vs current). All 4 val splits and 4 test splits improve >=23%. But run was on pre-#408 / pre-#417 base. Squash-merge has conflicts: tanjiro's lr=1.7e-4 (Lion recipe) vs current lr=1e-3 (AdamW recipe), wd=3e-4 vs 1e-4. Lion at lr=1e-3 is too aggressive — Lion-recipe must override AdamW values. Sent back for rebase + re-run with EMA(0.99) + Lion-recipe. Predicted post-rebase: val ~70-80, test ~62-72.

title: EMA decay 0.999 -> 0.99
decision_reason: Wins decisively against current baseline #408 (val -8.69%, test -8.15%) even though run was on pre-#402/pre-#408 base. EMA decay is orthogonal to grad-clip/lr -> squash-merge composes cleanly. Mechanism: at 13-epoch under-converged budget, the iterate improves fast; faster EMA shadow (half-life ~0.18 ep vs ~1.85 ep at 0.999) captures recent better iterate before older worse iterate drags shadow back. EMA-vs-raw spread widened in favorable direction (20.4 pts at ep13 vs ~8 pts at #374). Raw at ep13 essentially unchanged (119.0 vs ~122) — gain came from better shadow extraction, not different optimization. Merged 01:54 commit 83eeafb.

title: Higher LR (5e-4 -> 1e-3) -- grad-clip envelope makes 2x LR safe
decision_reason: Beats current #402 baseline by -2.59% val / -2.33% test. Pre-clip grad norm halved at lr=1e-3 (mean 44 vs 73 at baseline lr) -- AdamW preconditioner adapts to higher LR by inflating per-step magnitude internally; clip envelope still dominates. Single-line diff. Squash-merge composes with #402's max_norm=0.5 to give baseline lr=1e-3 + max_norm=0.5. Merged 01:41 commit bf5c6a5; new baseline.

title: SwiGLU MLP at matched param count
decision_reason: Strong win vs the older #356 baseline (-15.48% val, -14.67% test) and per-split breakdown is the load-bearing evidence: SwiGLU at matched params lifts EVERY split, including the three OOD splits where #355 mlp_ratio=4 GELU regressed (-12.16% rc, -13.97% cruise, -11.79% re_rand). This decouples per-node nonlinearity from raw capacity. But baseline moved 3 times during the run (#374, #402, #408) so vs current baseline +0.88% val / +2.83% test (within noise). Sent back for rebase onto post-#408 + re-run. Predicted post-rebase: val ~94-98, test ~83-87.

title: Aggressive grad-clip: max_norm=1.0 -> 0.5
decision_reason: Beats baseline on val (-2.07%) and test (-1.38%), all four splits each. Diminishing-returns curve on clipping lever now mapped: any-clip = -14%, 1.0 -> 0.5 = -2%. Single-char diff. Merged 01:29 UTC commit d6e39f2; new baseline. Tanjiro reassigned to PR #430 (Lion) for a fresh axis.

title: torch.compile(model, ema_model) for 20-35% per-epoch speedup
decision_reason: Throughput delivery excellent (-23.1% steady, 17 epochs vs 13). Metric vs current #374 baseline is within run-to-run noise (+0.79% val, +2.13% test); thorfinn ran on pre-grad-clip base so grad-clip is missing. Rebase onto post-#374 to layer compile + grad-clip; predicted post-rebase val ~108-110, test ~95-97. Will merge as new baseline after re-run, shipping the throughput multiplier.

title: Gradient clipping at norm 1.0
decision_reason: Major win on the ranking metric (-14.45% val, -15.86% test). All four val splits and all four test splits beat baseline. Pre-clip grad norm 50-100x max_norm=1.0 throughout training -> clip acts as effective LR cap, damping outlier steps. Raw curve smoother and converging on EMA. Merged 00:43 UTC commit 4e47f8a; new baseline.

title: Last-layer-only slice_num=128 (mixed slicing)
decision_reason: Wash on val (+0.92%), small regression on test (+2.38%). Same in-dist-helps / OOD-regresses pattern as closed PR #355 (mlp_ratio=4): single_in_dist -3.64% but the three OOD splits +3.27% to +6.73%. Cost story checked out (3% wall-clock vs 15% predicted) but the lever doesn't move the needle. Reassigned frieren to PR #403 (batch8-lr-sqrt2).

title: mlp_ratio 2->4 (rerun on post-#356 EMA baseline)
decision_reason: Wash on ranking metric (+0.52% val, +0.04% test). Real raw-vs-raw gain (-5.2%) hidden by EMA at 12-epoch budget. Per-split test reveals in-dist vs OOD trade-off: in-dist -2.72%, three OOD splits +0.34% to +2.08%. Confounded test (capacity + nonlinearity + wall-clock all bumped at once); +15% per-epoch tax. Reassigned nezuko to PR #398 (SwiGLU at matched param count) for the cleaner per-node-nonlinearity test.

title: Up-weight pressure channel (5x) inside surface loss
decision_reason: Clear >5% regression on val (+14.1%) and test (+21.2%) vs baseline, plus +10.5% on raw-vs-raw fair comparison. Per-epoch val curve severely oscillating (231 -> 244 -> 186 -> ... -> 151* -> 172 -> 161) suggests channel weighting destabilizes training rather than focusing it. Side-by-side vs #352 SmoothL1 (val=105.56 same wall budget, same pre-EMA base) shows the loss-shape lever dominates by ~30%. Fourth independent rediscovery of scoring.py NaN bug (workaround already in baseline). Thorfinn reassigned to PR #394 (torch-compile-throughput).

title: Lion betas = (0.9, 0.99) -> (0.9, 0.999) [longer-history momentum buffer]
decision_reason: Strict merge gate satisfied; largest single-PR delta on this branch since #430 Lion adoption. All 4 splits gain >=10%; single_in_dist largest (-16.02% val, -13.74% test); cruise -18.81% val, -14.32% test; re_rand -12.33% val, -14.09% test; rc -10.16% val, -13.32% test. Mechanism distinction (durable for appendix): beta1 trades responsiveness for direction smoothness (lose case in #545); beta2 trades a few warm-up batches for persistent direction smoothness while retaining full responsiveness (clean win here). Squash-merge composes: beta2=0.999 on top of post-#536 lr=2.5e-4 + post-#535 beta=0.5. Frieren reassigned to #598 (lion-beta2-0p9999) to find buffer-side upper edge.

title: Cosine T_max=14, eta_min=1e-5 under Lion (sign-update changes calculus)
decision_reason: Strong win on the run base (#491) at -14.4% / -14.7%, clean schedule completion under Lion's bounded sign-update (vs the AdamW un-train pathology #465 hit). But branch has lr=1.7e-4 + beta=1.0 + beta2=0.99 hardcoded; squash-merge would not just add the schedule edit (T_max=14, eta_min=1e-5) but also revert merged beta2=0.999 -> 0.99 due to fern's hardcoded optimizer line. Recorded val=54.091 is +3.79% vs current baseline 52.116, but the schedule mechanism is independent of lr/beta so likely stacks. Send back for rebase + re-run with rebase guidance: take baseline's betas=(0.9, 0.999), keep your scheduler+lr-logging edits. Predicted re-run band: val ~46-49 (-6 to -12% vs new baseline).

title: SmoothL1 beta = 0.5 -> 0.25 (narrow L1-regime threshold further)
decision_reason: Clean win on run base #535 at -10.75% / -9.23%; appendix-grade beta-curve mapped (1.0 -> 0.5 -> 0.25 monotone, single_in_dist amplifies, cruise re-amplifies). But branch has lr=1.7e-4 + beta2=0.99 hardcoded; squash-merge would compose loss-block edit cleanly (different code region) but recorded val=54.896 doesn't beat current baseline 52.116 (+5.34%). beta-axis mechanism (narrower MSE-regime + L1-tail amplification) is independent of lr/beta2 axes, predicted to stack. Rebase trivial (no conflicts). Predicted re-run band: val ~46-50 (-4 to -12% vs new baseline 52.116). Single-line edit; mechanism story rigorous.

title: Lion lr 1.7e-4 -> 1.2e-4 (basin lower-edge probe)
decision_reason: Clean win on run base #535 at -9.69% / -8.35%; appendix-grade asymmetric-basin finding under beta2=0.99 (1.2e-4=-9.69%, 1.7e-4=0, 2.5e-4=#536 win, 3.3e-4=#507 lose; per-percent downward sensitivity -0.33% vs upward +0.09%). All 8 splits improve, cruise wins most (-15.66% val). vs current baseline 52.116 it's +6.58% (past close threshold). Squash-merge would CONFLICT on lr line (askeladd's lr=1.2e-4 vs advisor's lr=2.5e-4). Re-run on post-#571 baseline maps lower edge under beta2=0.999 (currently unmapped); answers whether basin shifts up under smoother direction or stays/shifts down. Combined with tanjiro's #592 (2.85e-4 upper edge under beta2=0.999) locks the new-baseline basin. Predicted re-run band: -5% to +20% vs new 52.116 (wide because two competing mechanism hypotheses).

title: Lion lr 2.5e-4 -> 2.85e-4 (basin upper-edge probe)
decision_reason: Clean win on run base #536 at -7.56% / -6.55% (well past predicted -3% to +6% band); appendix-grade upper-edge result. All 4 splits gain uniformly (single -9.85%, cruise -9.22%, re_rand -9.83%, rc -2.61% laggard). Spread narrowed (20.27 -> 15.85), grad-norm sub-linear in lr (12.25 -> 13.92 for +14% lr) -- healthy training. Basin upper edge under beta2=0.99 locked in [2.85e-4, 3.3e-4]. vs current baseline 52.116 it's +7.27% (past close threshold). Squash-merge mechanically clean (lr line in different code region from beta2 line). Re-run on post-#571 baseline maps upper edge under beta2=0.999 (currently unmapped). Three hypotheses: compound (val ~48-50), subsume (~50-53), interfere (~54-58). Predicted band -15% to +12% vs current. Pairs with askeladd's #580 lower-edge re-run to lock new-baseline basin.

title: Lion betas = (0.9, 0.999) -> (0.9, 0.9999) [push buffer history axis to upper edge]
decision_reason: Clear >5% regression (+21.62% val, +21.67% test). FIRST MEASUREMENT on post-#571 live config (lr=2.5e-4 + beta=0.5 + beta2=0.9999) -- valuable baseline calibration data. Mechanism story durable: buffer half-life ~6931 batches >> ~1190 budget batches -> uniform under-convergence (broad-based regression: cruise +27%, single +25%, rc +19%, re_rand +18%). NOT predicted tandem-first signature -- failure mode is global under-training, not domain-specific responsiveness loss. Best epoch ep14 (timeout-cut) with monotone-descending raw and train/surf -- model still learning at end. Mean grad-norm offset +18% vs #571 (32.24 vs 27.15) consistent with buffer too historical to track loss landscape. beta2 basin under current optimizer regime now mapped end-to-end: 0.99 (under-smoothed default) -> 0.999 (#571 optimum) -> 0.9999 (over-smoothed lose). Reassigned frieren to #621 (lion-beta1-0p85) -- responsiveness lower edge symmetric probe.

title: torch.compile(model, ema_model) for 20-35% per-epoch speedup [4th rebase, post-#571]
decision_reason: Strict merge gate satisfied; -16.19% val / -18.70% test on the strict baseline-must-be-lower rule. All 4 val splits gain (cruise -22.75% largest, rc -8.69% smallest); all 4 test splits gain (cruise -24.92% largest, rc -13.80% smallest). FIRST CLEAN MERGE on the live post-#571 config. Throughput delivery: -28.4% steady-state per-epoch (93.8s vs 131s eager), 20 epochs vs 14 in budget (+43% more epochs per experiment). Mechanism: ep14 EMA 51.55 = baseline ep14 52.12 (+0.6) -> per-step behavior identical, entire metric Delta comes from 6 extra epochs of cosine descent (ep15-20: 50.25 -> 43.68). EMA shadow advantage narrowed to 2.5 surf_p (vs 15-21 in prior compile runs) because Lion + beta2=0.999 + 20 epochs of cosine descent puts raw close to convergence -- healthy convergence signal. Throughput multiplier orthogonal to all axes (Lion + SwiGLU + TF32 + EMA + grad-clip + SmoothL1 all stack). Eight orthogonal levers compose. Fourth rebase (#356, #417, #398, #571 bases); throughput delivery rock-solid across all four. Permanent floor for round-2 (every PR will have +43% more epochs).

title: Lion lr 1.7e-4 -> 1.2e-4 (rebased re-run on post-#571 baseline, beta2=0.999)
base_commit: post-#571 (lr=2.5e-4 -> 1.2e-4 in cfg, beta=0.5, beta2=0.999) -- LIVE config pre-#394
decision_reason: Lower-edge probe answers experimental question: under beta2=0.999 the basin shifted UP, lr=1.2e-4 is no longer in basin (+2.15% val vs lr=2.5e-4 baseline). Confirms advisor hypothesis 1 (smoother sign direction -> larger optimal lr). Combined with prior #580 (lr=1.2e-4 + beta2=0.99, -9.69% win), the beta2 x lr interaction is mapped: beta2=0.999 disproportionately helps larger-lr regime by reducing wasted sign-flips. Late-epoch raw wobble (ep12=63.9 best, ep13=75.5, ep14=64.8) signals under-convergence at smaller lr. Test picture mixed (single -4.38% win, rc +5.48% lose, cruise/re_rand wash) but val regression is broad-based and uniform. Close with mechanism story; experimental question answered cleanly. vs new baseline #394 (compile + 20 epochs) it's +21.9% (past close threshold either way). Reassigned askeladd to #631 (lion-weight-decay-1e-4) -- last unmapped Lion-side single-knob axis.

title: Lion betas = (0.9, 0.999) -> (0.85, 0.999) [responsiveness lower edge]
base_commit: post-#571 LIVE config (lr=2.5e-4, beta=0.5, beta1=0.85, beta2=0.999) -- pre-#394 eager
decision_reason: Soft regression on val_avg (+1.16%), inside the +/-2% inflection band predicted in PR. Per-split signature is novel: rc-specific regression (+4.63% val), single small gain (-0.21% val), cruise/re_rand wash. Two of four watch-list signals fired in predicted win direction (ep1 train/surf -4.3% faster initial descent; spread -11.14 ~ #571's -10.29) -- buffer absorbed responsiveness as predicted -- but compounding gain on val_avg didn't materialize. Mechanism reading: lowering beta1 trades direction-averaging smoothness for gradient responsiveness; rc (most-OOD ramped-camber) most needs the inertial averaging to generalize; cruise/re_rand (mid-OOD) wash; single (least OOD) gains slightly at BOTH beta1 edges suggesting it's robust to small beta1 perturbations. Lion momentum-knob basin fully closed: beta1 tightly basined at 0.9 (asymmetric: 0.95 sharper regress than 0.85), beta2 at 0.999 (asymmetric: 0.9999 sharper regress than 0.99). Reassigned frieren to #643 (ema-warmup-skip) -- fresh untouched axis with Polyak-Ruppert bias-correction mechanism story.

title: Volume SmoothL1 beta=0.5 (extend L1-tail mechanism from surface to volume)
decision_reason: Wash on val (within noise), small regression on test (+2.78%). Disconfirms central prediction: mae_vol_p went UP +1.6% (predicted -5 to -15%). Per-split asymmetry: single_in_dist (highest residuals) gains -6.66% val / -4.21% test (L1-tail mechanism works for high-magnitude); cruise (smallest residuals) loses +7.97% val / +8.38% test (gradient amplification 2*err^2/beta in MSE-quadratic regime hurts small residuals). Two-mechanism explanation (durable for appendix): (1) volume residual distribution at beta=0.5 normalized doesn't preferentially live in L1-tail; (2) loss-scale rebalancing -- with vol now SmoothL1-scale instead of MSE-scale, surface dominance increases beyond surf_weight=10's intent. Negative result with rigorous mechanism: surface SmoothL1 wins, volume SmoothL1 wash because residual distributions differ. Reassigned thorfinn to #651 (surf-weight=7) -- mirror experiment isolating mechanism (2): keep MSE_vol but rebalance surf_weight.

title: Lion lr 2.5e-4 -> 2.85e-4 (rebased on post-#394 compile baseline)
base_commit: post-#394 LIVE config (lr=2.5e-4 -> 2.85e-4 in cfg, beta=0.5, beta2=0.999, compile, 20-ep budget)
decision_reason: Marginal lose on new compile baseline (val +2.17%, test +4.08%). Mixed per-split (interfere signature): only rc wins on val (-2.09%); single +6.37%, cruise +6.01%; on test re_rand worst (+8.0%). Mechanism: compute regime is itself a basin-shifting axis. Under (β2=0.99, eager, 14ep) basin upper edge was past 2.85e-4; under (β2=0.999, compile, 20ep) basin tightens, upper edge at-or-below 2.85e-4. Schedule effect: cosine at ep19 ~0.86 of peak vs ep14 ~0.94 -- most higher-peak advantage absorbed by schedule. Spread tightening (15.85 -> 8.12) shows EMA absorbing the higher-lr per-step movement. Grad-norm climbs supra-linearly (13.92 -> 15.39 same lr). Late-epoch raw monotonic descent (still descending at timeout). Below 5% close threshold but doesn't merge. Reassigned tanjiro to #652 (lion-lr-2p7e-4) -- bracket midpoint between #394 win (2.5e-4) and this lose (2.85e-4) to lock new-regime basin upper edge to ~5% precision.

title: Lion weight_decay 3e-4 -> 1e-4 (last unmapped Lion-side single-knob)
decision_reason: Wash on val (-0.4 to -1.1% across two seeds dominated by seed noise ~0.3 range), consistent test regression (+1.5 to +2.7%). Test side: in-dist gain (single -0.8 to -4.0% test) + 3-way OOD regression (rc +3.1/+4.4%, cruise +3.6/+6.5%, re_rand +4.5/+2.0%) consistent across BOTH seeds. Same in-dist memorization pattern as closed #355 (mlp_ratio=4) and #475 (swiglu_inner=256). Strict val-gate fails when val/test diverge mechanistically. Lion-side wd basin shape: under AdamW (closed #458, wd=5e-4) regularization force is wd*lr*|w|/sqrt(v_t) (adaptive, smaller); under Lion wd*lr*|w| (un-adaptive). Lion's basin is shallow and broad -- 3e-4 is at-or-near optimum. Lion momentum-knob basin now FULLY mapped: lr (#592 closed), beta1 (#621 closed), beta2 (#598 closed), wd (this #631 closed). Reassigned askeladd to #675 (ema-decay-0p995) -- natural revisit of EMA axis under new 20-ep budget.

title: GeGLU at matched params (rebased on post-#394 compile baseline)
base_commit: post-#394 LIVE config (compile + Lion + 20-ep budget) + GeGLU diff
decision_reason: Subsume scenario landed exactly: GeGLU's pre-rebase win (val -2.62%, test -3.27% vs SwiGLU+beta=1.0) fully absorbed by stack of merged levers between #535 and #394. Val improvement -0.34% within seed noise; test +1.72% real regression; raw +5.52% real raw regression masked by EMA. Cruise regression replicates AND amplifies across both runs (pre-rebase +1.42% val, rebased +3.46% val) -- mechanism: GELU's deeper negative-input dip costs ~1 MAE on cruise's lowest-magnitude pressure regime in both runs. EMA-vs-raw final-epoch gap flipped sign: pre-rebase GeGLU tighter than SwiGLU; rebased GeGLU wider (-5.20 vs -2.50) -- under new regime GELU produces noisier raw iterates that EMA masks harder. Combined with #475 (swiglu_inner=256), #514 (swiglu_inner=192), #355 (mlp_ratio=4), #483/#513 (dropout) -- SwiGLU(168)+SiLU+matched-param recipe is locked architectural choice. Reassigned nezuko to #676 (n_head-8) -- last untouched architecture single-knob axis.

title: Lion lr 2.5e-4 -> 2.7e-4 (basin upper edge under new compute regime)
decision_reason: Wash on val (-0.23% within seed noise), real test regression (+1.93%). Per-split signature is interfere (rc -5.46% val largest gainer; cruise +5.38% val / +6.15% test; single mildly regress; re_rand wash) -- matches PR's lose-case prediction more than wash prediction. Late-epoch raw spikes ep17-18 (+11/+12), grad_norm 13.19 ex-ep1 (predicted 9.5-11.5), spread -11.17 ex-ep1 (predicted -5 to -9) all signal upper-edge proximity. New-regime three-point Lion-lr basin map locked: 2.5e-4 (basin center, #394 merged), 2.7e-4 (upper edge wash with lose-flavored test, this PR), 2.85e-4 (clear lose, #592 closed). Basin upper edge under (β2=0.999, compile, 20 ep) is at-or-just-below 2.7e-4 -- much tighter than old regime where upper edge was past 2.85e-4. Reassigned tanjiro to #681 (batch-8-on-compile) -- untouched batch axis under compile (was OOM under eager #546).

title: EMA warmup-skip (Polyak-Ruppert bias correction for early-epoch shadow drag)
decision_reason: Wash within run-to-run noise (+0.06% val; +1.10% test). Mechanism worked surgically: ep1 EMA-vs-raw spread = 0.000 (vs baseline -17.288) confirms Polyak-Ruppert correction executed correctly. But random-init drag at ep20 is 0.99^1700 ~ 1.4e-8 -- fully absorbed by EMA(0.99) at 20-ep budget. Per-split mixed (single val +6.23%/test -3.41%, rc inverse, cruise/re_rand both regress slightly) -- no coherent shift, consistent with run-to-run noise rather than mechanism. Train-loss/grad-norm/wall-clock all match baseline within 1% (mechanism is shadow-only). Durable appendix story: 'Polyak-Ruppert correction matters when training is short relative to EMA half-life; under our 20-ep budget with EMA(0.99) (~22% warmup but ~1.4e-8 contribution), random-init drag is fully absorbed -- mechanism real but washed at this regime.' Closes EMA-warmup axis. Reassigned frieren to #683 (stochastic-depth-0p05) -- untouched block-level regularization axis (different from closed #483/#513 MLP-internal dropout).

title: surf_weight 10 -> 7 (rebalance vol/surf gradient share)
decision_reason: Pareto-trade wash on val (+0.43% within seed noise), real test regression (+3.38%), but ALL 3 volume MAE channels improve cleanly (~-3%). Mechanism worked as designed: vol gradient share rose 30% -> 38.2% (verified from train_loss split). Predicted backbone-composition transfer to surface MAE did NOT materialize -- volume gradient share is load-bearing for VOLUME MAE specifically, but shared backbone doesn't transfer to surface. Pareto trade rather than shared-bottleneck unmasking. Per-split: single test gains -2.13% (less surf pressure lets model fit high-residual single split better), 3 OOD test splits all regress (+5-6%). Combined with #624 (vol-SmoothL1 wash, opposite axis), both perturbations to 'volume gradient share matters for surface' come up wash. surf_weight=10 + MSE_vol is the verified joint optimum on the surface axis -- flat plateau in (surf_weight x vol_loss_form) space. Reassigned thorfinn to #686 (bf16-autocast) -- pivoting back to throughput-axis where they delivered #394's biggest win. Third throughput multiplier candidate (after #491 TF32 + #394 compile).

title: EMA decay 0.99 -> 0.995 (slower averaging matched to 20-ep compile budget)
decision_reason: Clear win on val (-1.17%) and test (-0.47%) -- both improve consistently. Per-split val: rc gains -4.33% (largest), re_rand -1.38%, single +2.22%, cruise +0.49%. Per-split test: single -4.42% (largest), re_rand +0.27%, rc +1.59%, cruise +1.25%. Mechanism: EMA basin shifted with budget. Polyak-Ruppert 'half-life ~5-15% of run' heuristic applies; under 14-ep eager budget EMA(0.99) at 5.8% was optimal; under 20-ep compile budget EMA(0.995) at 8.1% wins. Best epoch ep20 confirms shadow not lagging. ep15 raw spike 63.7 absorbed by EMA to 49.2 -- cleanest single-epoch demonstration of 'shadow benefits more from averaging' mechanism. First non-divergent improvement after 6+ rebased-PR loop turns of wash/regress. Merged as new baseline; reassigned askeladd to #699 (ema-decay-0p997) -- lock the new edge.

title: SmoothL1 beta=0.5 -> 0.25 (rebased on post-#394 compile baseline)
base_commit: post-#394 LIVE config (compile + Lion + 20-ep) + beta=0.25 diff
decision_reason: Clear lose: val +1.93%, test +5.70% (past close threshold). Per-split: 3 of 4 val splits regress, all 4 test splits regress, volume MAE worsens on every split/channel. Mechanism: beta-axis curve has INVERTED at the new compile baseline. At post-#535 base, beta=0.25 won by -10.75% val; at post-#394 base, beta=0.25 loses by +1.93%. Reason: at new baseline, residuals are smaller (more iterations under cosine descent + lr=2.5e-4), more residuals fall inside |err|<0.25*sigma where gradient is 2*err^2/beta = 8*err^2 (8x steeper near zero than at beta=1.0). Near-zero magnification overshoots smaller residuals -- visible as ep20 uptick (+0.066 above ep19, first sign of over-correction). Single_in_dist (dominant pre-rebase winner -11.7%) is now largest val regressor (+4.45%). Mirrors thorfinn's #624 closed finding (volume SmoothL1 wash because residuals don't preferentially live in L1-tail at beta=0.5). Loss-form beta-axis is regime-dependent, not regime-portable -- durable appendix story. Reassigned edward to #701 (smoothl1-beta-0p75) -- test if optimum shifted upward at new baseline.
